title: Nested zones, boundaries, and rendering quality
description: Complete nested zone/boundary rendering and dense-diagram quality work: zone hierarchy display, boundary nesting, overlay conflict priority, badge collapse/inspector handoff, edge/label legibility hardening, and acceptance examples.
triage_notes: IN PROGRESS. Cut from product principles rendering quality plus 03 zone view/nesting and overlay readability sections. 2026-06-27 Codex partial: fixed observed comprehensive-sample label overlap where zone labels (e.g. OnPrem/On-premises) collided with member nodes by reserving 36px top header padding for zone/boundary boxes and widening lane gap to keep adjacent boxes separated. Added layout regression test for zone label header clearance. Actual comprehensive sample OnPrem headerGap now 36px. 2026-06-27 Codex partial: added connection-density-aware component sizing in render layout. Node size now grows from graph edge degree plus permission overlay relationships so high-degree hubs get more internal padding and more port distribution room while low-degree nodes stay compact. Actual comprehensive sample CloudRun is now rendered as the largest hub size while GKECluster/Worker remain compact. 2026-06-27 Codex partial: changed permission overlay rendering from center-to-center straight lines to side-aware orthogonal paths with distributed source/target ports and stacked target-side labels. 2026-06-27 Codex correction: dense permission overlays now collapse long role labels and role badges into short in-node permission summaries; actual comprehensive sample permission overlay has 0 rendered roles/... texts and 9 permission summaries. 2026-06-27 Codex correction: normal graph edges incident to high-degree components now use balanced side-aware routing instead of concentrating on one or two faces; actual comprehensive sample CloudRun edges now use left 4 / right 3 / top 4 / bottom 4 endpoints. 2026-06-27 Codex correction: reciprocal/multi-edge pairs between the same two components now route on separate outside tracks instead of reusing the same line; Web->FirebaseAuth uses the upper track while FirebaseAuth->Web uses the lower track in the human-provided LR sample. 2026-06-27 Codex partial: viewer/playground settings now use the product taxonomy Views (overview/zone/layer), Render modes (2d/isometric/3d), and Overlays (auth/dataflow/boundary/permission/validation). Added layer as a registered semantic 2D view using layer-ranked layout; render-mode is accepted by render() and <archmap-viewer>. 2026-06-27 Codex partial: implemented renderMode=isometric as a station-map-style movable SVG 2.5D projection, not the 3D fallback. It renders zones as floor polygons, nodes as shallow extruded boxes, and projected edges/overlays; because it is SVG output, existing pan/zoom interaction applies. Completion: nested modeled zones/boundaries render legibly; dense diagrams remain inspectable; no edge-over-node/shared-endpoint regressions in tests/examples. Remaining: nested zone hierarchy display, boundary nesting quality, selected highlight, diagnostics-to-diagram focus, broader non-permission edge-label collision handling, optional richer isometric controls (angle/depth/layer visibility).
